payload.author: claude
payload.body:
AGENT BRIEFING (read before investigating).

FULL CONTEXT: read epic SCP-120 and validation spike SCP-121 first (thesis = agent-operated multiplayer kanban; the relay is what makes the agent-driven board multiplayer).

WHY THIS MATTERS: the status-claim events that prevent agent collisions must reach every subscriber with minimal latency, across app servers. The in-process EventEmitter bus dies the moment we run >1 app server.

WHERE TO LOOK:
- src/events.js (in-process EventEmitter bus — what must become external pub/sub)
- src/server.js (the /events SSE endpoint ~lines 450-485; keepalive; workspace filtering)
- src/hub.js (watchdog, port-race self-healing, Bonjour discovery — becomes LB + failover + health checks; document what we drop)
- App/Sources/Network/EventStream.swift and the web EventSource in src/web/app.js

STILL OPEN (your job): external pub/sub choice (Redis pub/sub vs NATS vs Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY vs managed bus) + tradeoffs; keep SSE vs move to WebSocket (call out the SSE-over-WAN reconnect unknown); per-tenant/per-project topic routing; connection scale per node + LB handling of long-lived conns. Coordinate w/ SCP-124 (appends are the event source — likely LISTEN/NOTIFY or an outbox row) and SCP-126 (can the chosen runtime hold long-lived connections?).

DELIVERABLE: comment a proposed fan-out architecture here, then spawn implementation child stories with parent=SCP-120.
